sender.notes: Soldier awaiting discharge; first name/surname not on these pages

letters.stationery: Army and Navy YMCA — With the Colors
letters.summary: April 1919 letter on Army & Navy YMCA stationery from a soldier awaiting discharge near Nashville to Blanche — mail, exams, discharge papers, and plans to reunite.
letters.context: Written just after World War I on “With the Colors” YMCA paper. The writer has signed discharges and hopes to catch a train home within days.
letters.notes: Post-Armistice demobilization letter (April 1919). Page 1 is the dated greeting to Blanche; page 2 is the affectionate close. Filename stem 19190410bnpg suggests date + initials; confirm against envelope if available.
letters.reading:
April 10-1919

Dearest Darling Blanche:

I received your letter that you wrote Tuesday yesterday morning about nine o'clock and it was stamped at 8 30 Tuesday night in Nashville so it sure did make a quick trip didn't it. We past our exams yesterday and signed our discharges and also the pay roll last night and they say we will get our discharge and all out if nothing happens and I sure hope we get them soon this morning so I can catch #2 or the

Hope flies out so I can get [illegible] Nashville Sat. eve so we can fly together Sat. night and we can go out home Sunday. Just think only three more days and then we can love each other and just keep on loving each other and not have to be studying about when we have to depart again.

Oh Honey I just want you to always love me and when I am tired or feeling bad I will be perfectly happy if you will just put your arms around me and kiss me.

Hope flies out so I can get [illegible] Nashville Sat. eve so we can fly together Sat. night and we can go out home Sunday. Just think only three more days and then we can love each other and just keep on loving each other and not have to be studying about when we have to depart again.

Oh Honey I just want you to always love me and when I am tired or feeling bad I will be perfectly happy if you will just put your arms around me and kiss me.

sender.notes: User identified writer as uncle Franny; family-tree top box reads Frans

recipient.notes: General family update / circular, not a single addressed recipient

letters.summary: Undated family circular from Uncle Franny — a hand-drawn family tree plus short updates on relatives in California, Texas, Missouri, and the East Coast.
letters.context: A cheerful multi-page newsletter-style letter covering careers, pets, travel, and engagements across the extended family.
letters.notes: Modern family circular by Uncle Franny. Opens with family tree (Frans & Nancy → Lea, Auko, Ingrid, Jorgen) then geographic updates: California, Texas, Missouri, then East Coast (New Haven, Cape Cod, Boston).
letters.reading:
. . . What family is all about

A brief description follows with names, updates and locations . . .

[Family tree: Frans — Nancy; children Lea, Auko, Ingrid, Jorgen; under Lea: Frans, Nicole, Rachel; under Auko: Frans; under Jorgen: Taylor]

California
Ingrid who worked in advertising in Los Angeles and Japan with Japan Airlines now works as a vet-technician in Simi Valley. She has a preference for larger animals. "Little Bear", never one to be intimidated by size . . . [illegible]. He's a Jack Russel Terrier.
Leslie and daughter Taylor can be spotted on their daily jogging routine in Santa Monica. Jorgen balances his legal profession at Toyota and travels to Japan with hands-on work around the house. His speciality combines exotic projects, tools and cacti. "Big Blue and Buffie" (Bouvier de Flandres) are happy to have little Taylor (Scottie) as a new member of the family.

On to Texas
"Big Bob" has been discovered to have a talent for the "Texas Two Step" in addition to being #1 Representative of his Company in the entire USA. Lea is doing well in her field of Interior Design for Retirement Living Facilities in Japan. Rachel does well in school and shines as a "Star Duster" at junior sports events and Senior Citizen Centers in the Austin metropolitan area. Heidi a Dutch Keeshond and three rabbits round out the family.

up in Missouri
Big Frans is quite comfortable in a red enameled Adirondack Chair of his own design and making. Lorna his fiancée can lay claim to the longest rambling ivy west of the Mississippi.

Missouri Continued
They recently celebrated their engagement at the Racquet Club in St. Louis. Each are practicing lawyers.. Frans in St. Louis and Lorena in Springfield Illinois. Their kitten from Texas (Guess from who) is a new addition to the household.

Moving to the East Coast

New Haven:
Nicole and Eugene find their lives pretty much involved with the many opportunities Yale has to offer. After their travel to St. Louis for Frans and Lorena's engagement party they now look forward to visiting Cape Cod.. then Mexico City and Acapulco to visit Eugene's family. Nicole is with the Yale Endowment Office.

Cape Cod
Frans Jr. (Aako) is now fully occupied juggling his work in Cape Cod and classes at the Cambridge School of Culinary Arts in Boston where he will graduate as *"Chef de Cuisine"* [head chef]. Laurie provides an extra dimension to the picture. She recently made family history by participating in the Annual New York [illegible] AIDS Ride from Boston to New York. Laurie’s biggest thrill... pedalling down 5th Avenue!

Boston
Another Frans (HoTo) has his hands full with his chosen career in the Film Making Industry as a gaffer (lighting). An independent, he has fortunately been kept busy and his network of contacts continues to grow. Patricia is an Admissions Counselor at Emmanuel College in Boston (She is an excellent Salsa Dancer) They share their time with "Grindle and Cleo", Siberian Huskies. Frans and Patricia travel extensively, both stateside and Latin America.
